title: PTD Today — Media

items.title: Would you go to an in-person dating app event?

ai.summary: Based on the available description, Gen Z are increasingly struggling with loneliness, and dating app Hinge announced a $1 million fund to support social groups in New York, Los Angeles and London to put on free or affordable social events for young people. The move is a global expansion of Hinge's One More Hour social impact initiative.
bullets: Hinge announced a $1 million fund to support social groups.
Fund targets New York, Los Angeles and London.
Purpose is to fund free or affordable social events for young people.
Effort responds to rising loneliness among Gen Z.
This is a global expansion of the One More Hour initiative.
takeaways: Hinge's event funding is focused on NYC, LA and London—operators in those markets should note potential event-driven demand spikes.
Organized social events for young people can create concentrated, short-duration needs for reliable venue connectivity and power—consider temporary capacity or rapid-response plans.
Partnerships between major apps and local groups may produce predictable event schedules—coordinate with local venues and organizers for capacity planning.
Monitor similar social-impact initiatives by large platforms when updating urban load forecasts and infrastructure readiness.

items.title: Build Knowledge Graph-powered AI Agents For Your Use Case | Databases for AI

ai.summary: Based on the available description… This session shows how to automatically build a knowledge graph from raw documents and connect it to an AI agent so the agent can understand a company's specific business. No graph expertise required — the presenters demonstrate an end-to-end build.
bullets: AI agents struggle with company-specific knowledge out of the box.
Knowledge graphs are presented as the solution to give agents business context.
The session demonstrates automatically building a knowledge graph from raw documents.
The graph is then connected to an agent so it can answer questions about your company.
No prior graph query or graph theory expertise is required; an end-to-end build is shown.
takeaways: Make internal documentation and operational data directly useful to AI agents for faster, context-aware responses.
Automated graph-building can lower the barrier to adopting knowledge-graph–powered agents without hiring specialized graph engineers.
Connecting a knowledge graph to agents can improve the relevance of AI-driven insights for infrastructure and operations decisions.
An end-to-end approach reduces integration overhead, accelerating pilot projects and time-to-value for AI initiatives.

items.title: Leveraging tax credits and incentives for sustainability projects

ai.summary: Based on the available description, when leaders think about tax credits for sustainability, energy incentives often come to mind but they’re only part of the picture. Randa Barsoum, Tax Partner at PwC US, highlights less obvious sources—state and local abatements, location-based incentives, and R&D tax credits tied to innovations like reimagining packaging or processes—and emphasizes that viewing sustainability investments through a broader lens can unlock meaningful tax value.
bullets: Energy incentives are commonly considered but not the only tax opportunities for sustainability.
State and local abatements can support sustainability initiatives.
Location-based incentives offer additional tax value tied to site decisions.
R&D tax credits can apply to innovation efforts, including reimagining packaging or processes.
Viewing sustainability investments through a broader tax lens can unlock meaningful value.
takeaways: Don’t limit planning to energy incentives; evaluate the full set of tax credits and incentives.
Factor state and local abatements and location-based incentives into site selection and cost models.
Consider R&D tax credit potential when pursuing process or operational innovations.
Treat sustainability investments as potential tax-value drivers in financial and strategic planning.

items.title: Bringing In $251K A Year With 3 Jobs In The Bay Area

ai.summary: Based on the available description, Naseema McElroy, 44, is a labor and delivery nurse in Brentwood, California who also runs a personal finance brand. She documented her journey paying off nearly $1 million in debt (incurred by 2015), started the business Financially Intentional, was debt-free by 2017, and now brings in $251K a year across three jobs; this profile is part of CNBC Make It's Millennial Money series.
bullets: Naseema McElroy is a 44-year-old labor and delivery nurse and founder of Financially Intentional.
She accrued nearly $1 million in debt by 2015 and documented her debt-payoff journey.
By 2017 she was debt-free after implementing new money-management strategies.
She now earns $251K per year through three jobs in the Bay Area.
This story appears in CNBC Make It's Millennial Money series.
takeaways: Use the $251K-annual, three-job data point when benchmarking total compensation expectations for Bay Area hires.
Consider offering or promoting financial-wellness resources — employees with significant past debt may value support and education.
Recognize and accommodate employees with side businesses or multiple jobs when designing scheduling and HR policies.
Highlight support for entrepreneurial activities and flexible work options in recruitment and retention materials to attract multi-income talent.

items.title: GeForce Garage - Summer of RTX Community Build

ai.summary: Based on the available description… GeForce Garage announces the winner of the Summer of RTX r/buildapc Community Build Contest—u/JeffersonPutnam—who put together a small-form-factor GeForce RTX 5090 rig. The video description lists the full winning system specs (with a PCPartPicker link), names GeForce host Dwight, and links to GeForce social channels.
bullets: Winner: u/JeffersonPutnam (Summer of RTX r/buildapc Community Build).
Winning system: SFF GeForce RTX 5090 rig in a Fractal Design Era 2 chassis.
Key components: GeForce RTX 5090 Founders Edition, Ryzen 7 9800X3D, MSI MPG B850I Edge, 32GB DDR5-6000, Corsair SF1000 PSU, Corsair Nautilus 240 AIO.
Full parts list available on PCPartPicker: https://pcpartpicker.com/list/hxnCRV.
GeForce host Dwight is credited (Twitter link) and GeForce social channels are promoted.
takeaways: The build demonstrates use of a top-tier GeForce RTX 5090 Founders Edition in a compact SFF chassis—note power and cooling needs when deploying high-performance GPUs in small enclosures.
Specified components (SF1000 PSU, Nautilus 240 AIO) highlight the importance of robust power delivery and liquid cooling for high-end consumer GPU installations.
A publicly shared PCPartPicker configuration makes the exact build reproducible for testing or benchmarking purposes.
Community-driven showcases and social promotion are used to surface specific GPU configurations and may inform outreach or demo strategies.

items.title: TTEC accelerates AI deployment and revenue streams with Google Cloud Consulting

ai.summary: Based on the available description, TTEC partnered with Google Cloud Consulting to rapidly prototype and deploy AI solutions that unlocked entirely new revenue streams. Sreeni Alakappan, VP of App Development and Automation at TTEC, explains how the company used GenAI and Google Cloud's infrastructure to streamline operations and boost developer productivity. Google Cloud Consulting helped compress TTEC's digital transformation timeline and expand the ambitions of what’s possible for their business.
bullets: TTEC collaborated with Google Cloud Consulting to accelerate AI prototyping and deployment.
GenAI and Google Cloud infrastructure were used to streamline operations and increase developer productivity.
Efforts unlocked entirely new revenue streams for TTEC.
Google Cloud Consulting compressed the company’s digital transformation timeline.
Partnership expanded what TTEC considers achievable for its business.
takeaways: Engage cloud consulting to shorten AI proof-of-concept-to-production timelines and speed time-to-value.
Leverage scalable cloud infrastructure to boost developer productivity and operational efficiency.
Consider GenAI-driven initiatives as potential new revenue sources rather than solely cost-saving projects.
Plan data center and capacity strategies to support faster, larger-scale cloud deployments enabled by consulting-led accelerations.
